title: Persistence via Cron Job
story: File integrity monitoring has detected a modification to /etc/cron.d/.
A new cron entry was added by an account that should not have write access
This is a common attacker technique: establish persistence by registering a
malicious scheduled task that survives reboots and evades manual inspection.
Identify the malicious cron entry, its schedule, and the payload it executes.

objectives.label: Identify the filename of the malicious cron file
What is the cron schedule expression?
What script/command does the cron job execute?
Which user account created the cron entry?

hints: Check both /etc/cron.d/ and user crontab events in the logs
File writes to /tmp/ during the same session are suspicious
The cron schedule uses @reboot or a specific minute/hour pattern
Correlate cron log events with the auth log to find who was logged in
splunk_queries: index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case6_* cron | sort _time | table _time, sourcetype, _raw
index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case6_* (/tmp/ OR crontab OR cron.d) | sort _time
